description: Regulatory watchlist for prediction markets, DeFi, and stablecoin regulation.

entities.name: CFTC

recent_actions.action: Director of Enforcement David Miller announced at NYU Law School that insider trading laws apply to all prediction markets under CEA Section 6(c)(1) and Rule 180.1 (misappropriation theory). Five enforcement priority areas: insider trading in prediction markets, market manipulation, market abuse/disruptive trading, retail fraud, willful AML/KYC violations. This is the CFTC's first explicit enforcement posture on prediction market insider trading.

Published ANPR (Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking) for prediction markets. Seeking public comment on principles-based framework addressing manipulation, asymmetric information, and sensitive event categories.
Issued Staff Advisory Letter No. 26-08 signaling supportive stance toward event-based derivatives including sports contracts.
Chairman Selig announced four-part pro-event-contract agenda. Withdrew 2024 proposed rule that would have banned political/sports contracts.

entities.risk_to_lever: low from CFTC directly. CFTC sees prediction markets as legitimate commodities instruments.

entities.name: US Congress (Democrats)

recent_actions.action: DEATH BETS Act introduced (bicameral: Rep. Mike Levin, Sen. Adam Schiff). Would ban CFTC-registered platforms from listing contracts on terrorism, assassination, war, or death.
BETS OFF Act introduced (Sens. Hickenlooper, Murphy). Bans wagering on government actions, terrorism, war, assassination.
STOP Corrupt Bets Act introduced (Raskin, Merkley). Bans prediction markets on elections, government actions, sports, military.
Prediction Markets Are Gambling Act introduced (Sen. Schiff bipartisan with Sen. Curtis). Would effectively destroy most of Kalshi and Polymarket's business.

entities.risk_to_lever: medium. If war/geopolitics contracts are banned on US-regulated platforms, LEVER's oracle sources become thinner. Republican-controlled Congress makes passage unlikely in near term.

entities.name: State AGs (39-state coalition)

recent_actions.action: Washington state filed civil lawsuit against Kalshi alleging illegal gambling.
Arizona AG Kris Mayes filed criminal charges against Kalshi. First-ever criminal charges against a prediction market.
11 state cease-and-desist orders against Kalshi. 8+ state lawsuits. Coalition claims $600M in lost tax revenue.

entities.risk_to_lever: low direct. LEVER is DeFi-native, not a CFTC-registered DCM. State AG enforcement targets Kalshi specifically (US-regulated entity). However, if Kalshi is forced to delist markets, LEVER loses the secondary oracle source.

entities.name: GENIUS Act / Stablecoin Regulation

recent_actions.action: GENIUS Act signed. First US federal stablecoin law. 100% reserve backing, monthly disclosures, full audit for >$50B issuers.
Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed final rules by July 2026.
Tether hired KPMG (full audit) and PwC (internal systems). USDT: $185B reserves, 80%+ in US Treasury Bills. Dramatically improved transparency.

entities.risk_to_lever: low near-term. USDT remains the primary collateral. Tether compliance improving. Risk if USDT is restricted for US users post-July 2026 rules.

entities.name: MiCA (EU)

recent_actions.action: USDT faces EU restrictions if Tether does not obtain MiCA authorization by deadline. Only USDC is MiCA-compliant among top-10 stablecoins.

entities.risk_to_lever: low near-term (LEVER not targeting EU users initially). Medium term if EU expansion is planned and USDT is restricted.
